                                     ANNEX 9. ADDITIONAL LESSONS LEARNED


Considerations for youth employment programs:

   •   From an efficiency perspective, pairing consumption smoothening with the provision of productive assets
       leads to higher impact. As the efficiency analysis shows, the Project combined two components that typically
       serve different purposes (public works aim to smoothen consumption and entrepreneurship support activities
       mean to enhance beneficiaries’ future income gains) in such a way that it led to an increase in each
       component’s individual efficiency. The estimated social rate of return of between 53 and 88 percent that the
       Project generated was significantly higher than that of similar interventions.

   •   In order to be effective, mentoring for business/entrepreneurial development should be tailored to the
       context where beneficiaries will launch their micro-enterprises. The Togo Employment Opportunities for
       Vulnerable Youth Project relied on local coaches and on the identification of actual opportunities and
       marketing strategies that would be relevant as participants establish or scale up their business in their village
       or area. Local coaches would then ensure periodic follow-up of the income generating activity to support young
       entrepreneurs addressing arising challenges.

Considerations linked to the public works component:

   •   Interventions that aim to create durable assets for the community must carefully consider how to ensure
       the availability of (human and material) resources required for their maintenance at the village level after
       the project ends. This includes making sure that participants receive training on maintenance of infrastructure
       and non-participants are willing to contribute to their conservation because they are aware of assets’
       contribution to the community’s livelihoods – an issue that emerged during the Project implementation.
       Strategies towards asset maintenance also include building warehouses to store the materials and equipment
       used to carry out the works, transferring the material resources acquired within the Project to village
       development committees, or supporting villages in organizing on-site maintenance works after the
       intervention ends.

   •   Participation into public works and the related activities may yield social cohesion within villages. In the
       Employment Opportunities for Vulnerable Youth Project, participation in community sub-projects and training
       sessions led to regular interactions among beneficiaries, triggering a sense of closeness particularly among
       women, who are traditionally more isolated. This was confirmed by several participants during the focus
       groups held in the framework of the ICR mission (please see Annex 7).

   •   Additional support provided to improve participation of women has proven effective in triggering positive
       gender dynamics beyond the intervention. Anecdotal evidence gathered through the ICR mission suggests
       that women’s participation in the Project, which was facilitated by the provision of ad hoc support (such as
       informal childcare to ensure women could take part in the community work and training), led to enhanced
       self-confidence and increased respect within the household, and women’s more proactive role within the
       household and the community.

     The World Bank
    Employment Opportunities for Vulnerable Youth Project (P157036)




Considerations with respect to program design and implementation:

   •   Reduced fragmentation of interventions can improve efficiency of labor market programs. As indicated in
       the Social Protection and Jobs chapter of the 2021 Togo Public Expenditure, existing labor market programs in
       the country are highly fragmented, with low number of beneficiaries and small budgets, which decreases their
       potential impacts. By combining different approaches to youth employment, the Togo Employment
       Opportunities for Vulnerable Youth Project reduced program fragmentation demonstrating high efficiency,
       which generates important lessons for future active labor market programming in Togo.

   •   Delays in one component can affect progress of other components, particularly if components are
       sequenced. In the experience of the Togo Employment Opportunities for Vulnerable Youth Project, delays in
       implementation of the sub-project components (Component 1) affected beneficiaries’ possibility to meet the
       pre-requisites for accessing funding under Component 2. Delays may be caused by slowness in preparation
       work (for example lengthy procurement procedures, recruitment of PIU staff, or due to challenging
       bureaucracy for signing MoUs with partners, among others) or to oversights in design if this fails to consider
       possible local-level challenges, as mentioned above.

abstract: Ratings for the Employment Opportunities for Vulnerable Youth Project for Togo were as follows: outcome was highly satisfactory, Bank performance was satisfactory, and monitoring and evaluation (M and E) quality was substantial. Some lessons learned included: programs that integrate both the demand and the supply side prove effective in promoting youth employment. An intervention that promotes savings alongside the enhancement of skills not only allows participants to start or expand an entrepreneurial activity, but it is likely to enhance its sustainability over time by facilitating re-investments. The provision of functional literacy within business-development training programs is critical in contexts of widespread analphabetism. Given the successful implementation of the Togo Employment Opportunities for Vulnerable Youth Project and the (implementation) capacities developed within National Community Development Support Agency (ANADEB), the intervention should be considered for scale up. Responsive task management and attentive supervision can rapidly address emerging contextual issues by adapting coordination and implementation arrangements. Implementation strategies that rely on synergies with available actors allow to save resources in terms of time and funding.
